preparation_method: vanilla syrup added (reduced quantity for less-sweet variant)
allergens: may contain traces of dairy depending on preparation environment

serving.description: 1 grande cup (473 ml / approximately 400 g beverage weight after ice)
serving.natural_language_portion: One grande (16 fl oz / 473 ml) cup as served at Starbucks Singapore, weighing approximately 400 g including ice dilution.

nutrient_source.title: Starbucks Vanilla Oatmilk Iced Shaken Latte — Nutrition Information (Grande, Less Sweet / Reduced Syrup)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central — Branded Foods; cross-referenced with Starbucks published nutrition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Starbucks publishes per-serving nutrition for the standard Vanilla Oatmilk Iced Shaken Latte (Grande). The 'Less Sweet' variant uses approximately half the standard vanilla syrup pumps (2 pumps instead of 4), reducing sugar and energy. Per-100g values are derived by dividing the adjusted grande serving values by the estimated serving weight of 400 g. Standard grande: ~200 kcal, 4 g protein, 4 g fat, 35 g carbohydrate, 30 g sugar. Less-sweet adjustment reduces sugar by approximately 30–35%, yielding ~165 kcal, 4 g protein, 4 g fat, 27 g carbohydrate, 21 g sugar per serving. Per-100g values calculated accordingly. USDA FoodData Central branded entries for oat-milk lattes used as cross-reference for macro plausibility.

gi_source.title: Oat milk glycemic index — peer-reviewed estimate; Oatly oat drink GI ~39 (Wolever et al., 2003, American Journal of Clinical Nutrition); espresso and vanilla syrup contribution modelled
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Starbucks Vanilla Oatmilk Iced Shaken Latte. GI estimated at 39 based on oat milk as the primary glycemic contributor (GI ~39 per University of Sydney GI Database for oat-based milk beverages). Vanilla syrup (sucrose-based) raises effective GI slightly; however, the reduced-syrup (less-sweet) formulation limits this effect. Espresso contributes negligible carbohydrate. Composite GI estimated at 39 (low). Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (6.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 4) × 39 / 100 = 27.2 g available carbohydrate × 0.39 ≈ 10.6, rounded to 10.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~140 mg per grande serving)
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 140 mg sodium per grande serving, this beverage is within acceptable limits for hypertensive individuals when consumed occasionally, but daily consumption should be monitored as part of total dietary sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar from vanilla syrup and naturally occurring sugars in oat milk
type2_diabetes.guidance: The less-sweet variant reduces added sugar compared to the standard formulation (approximately 21 g sugar per grande serving), but the oat-milk base contributes beta-glucan and a low GI (~39), which moderates postprandial glucose response; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for the ~27 g total carbohydrate per serving within their daily carbohydrate budget.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, plant-based milk base with beta-glucan
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The oat-milk base provides beta-glucan, a soluble fibre associated with LDL cholesterol reduction; saturated fat is low (~0.4 g per grande serving), making this beverage a relatively heart-friendly coffee option when consumed without additional high-fat accompaniments.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Vanilla Oatmilk Iced Shaken Latte (Less Sweet) is a cold espresso-based beverage sold at Starbucks Singapore. It is prepared by combining freshly pulled espresso shots with oat milk and a reduced quantity of vanilla syrup (approximately 2 pumps instead of the standard 4), then vigorously shaking the mixture over ice to create a frothy, chilled texture before serving in a cup filled with ice. The result is a smooth, lightly sweet, creamy coffee drink with a subtle vanilla aroma and the characteristic mild, slightly earthy sweetness of oat milk. The oat-milk base gives the beverage a silkier mouthfeel compared to black iced coffee, while the shaking process incorporates air to produce a light foam layer. Nutritionally, a grande (400 g) serving provides approximately 165 kcal, 4 g protein, 4 g fat, and 27 g carbohydrate, with around 21 g of sugar — meaningfully lower than the standard variant. The oat-milk base contributes beta-glucan, a soluble dietary fibre associated with improved glycaemic response and LDL cholesterol reduction. The glycaemic index is estimated at 39 (low), making it a more moderate glycaemic choice among sweetened coffee beverages. Sodium content is modest (~140 mg per serving). The beverage is dairy-free and suitable for lactose-intolerant individuals and vegans. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should account for the carbohydrate load (~27 g per serving) within their daily plan, while the low GI and beta-glucan content offer some mitigation of postprandial glucose spikes. It is available at Starbucks outlets across Singapore, including the One Fullerton location.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from Starbucks published nutrition data for the standard grande variant, adjusted for the reduced-syrup less-sweet formulation, and cross-referenced against USDA FoodData Central branded oat-milk latte entries. No exact per-100g entry for this specific less-sweet variant exists in a primary nutritional database. GI is an analogous estimate based on oat-milk GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: Per-100g values are calculated from an estimated 400 g grande serving weight (including ice dilution). The 'Less Sweet' variant uses approximately 2 pumps of vanilla syrup versus 4 in the standard version, reducing sugar by approximately 30–35%. Sentinel value documentation for all -1 fields: vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the available branded nutrition data for this beverage. Trans fat is 0.0 (confirmed zero by product formulation for plant-based oat-milk beverages). Cholesterol is 0.0 (confirmed zero; oat milk is plant-based and contains no cholesterol). GI set to 39 based on analogous oat-milk beverage data from the University of Sydney GI Database; this is an analogous estimate, not a direct measurement. Serving size of 400 g is an estimate for a grande (473 ml) cup accounting for ice displacement and dilution. The food is classified under Beverages rather than Mixed as it is a liquid coffee drink. Aliases consistency verified: all entries in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.
